description: Architect's raise-half output — cross-role corrections + global rule proposals. Both arrays default empty; silence is allowed. Rejection of lead corrections / lead-raised rules lives in the parallel lead_validate call.

corrections.description: Cross-role corrections — things no single role's lead could see.

action_id.description: Required for fix and remove. Role is encoded in the prefix.

task_id.description: Required for missing — the task the new action should belong under.

for_role.description: Required for missing — the role_tag that should own the new action.

correction.description: Concise prose (1–3 sentences) stating the break, citing the relevant ids/rules, and naming the change.

alternatives.description: 1–3 other viable approaches you considered — genuinely different choices, not rephrasings of the same proposal.

examples.title: Cross-role corrections and / or global rules to add

corrections.correction: Add a /health endpoint healthcheck so the dev container fails fast when the page is not served.
Drop the explicit width: 100vw on .canvas; it overrides the responsive sizing the backend service depends on.

rules.proposal: Static-only deliverables ship with no third-party network requests in the served HTML.
alternatives: Network requests allowed only when the action lists the host as a stack dependency.

examples.title: Nothing crosses role boundaries (iteration is ready as drafted)
